reasons: Kaja can suppress and drag one target into Lunox's burst range.
Lunox can use Darkening and Chaos Assault follow-up quickly once Kaja fixes the target position.

proof.summary: Kaja locks and drags the target, while Lunox uses her burst window to quickly punish the suppressed enemy.
worksBestWhen: Kaja catches a priority damage dealer or fragile target.
Lunox has Darkening and Chaos damage ready before Kaja commits.
failureCases: Kaja pulls the target away from Lunox's range.
Lunox is forced to use Brilliance defensively before the pickoff.
The target survives the burst and receives peel from teammates.

reasons: Kaja's suppression gives Saber a reliable target window for Triple Sweep.
Saber's single-target burst helps finish the enemy before Kaja's control window ends.

proof.summary: Kaja isolates and drags a target, then Saber adds single-target burst and airborne control to secure the pickoff.
worksBestWhen: Kaja suppresses a squishy or high-value target.
Saber waits until Kaja has committed before using Triple Sweep.
failureCases: Kaja catches a tank instead of a damage dealer.
Saber is too far away to follow up.
Enemy peel interrupts Saber before he reaches the target.

reasons: Kaja can drag a target into Eudora's burst range.
Eudora adds stun and high magic burst after Kaja starts the pickoff.

proof.summary: Kaja provides forced target access, while Eudora converts the caught target into a fast magic burst kill.
worksBestWhen: Kaja pulls the target toward Eudora.
Eudora saves her burst for the suppressed target.
failureCases: Eudora uses burst before Kaja confirms the catch.
The target survives with defensive items or team peel.
Kaja and Eudora lack vision before engaging.

reasons: Kaja's suppression can hold or drag a target into Kadita's delayed knock-up and burst path.
Kadita can punish the position Kaja creates instead of needing to start the combo alone.

proof.summary: Kaja fixes the target position, while Kadita layers delayed control and burst onto the pulled enemy.
worksBestWhen: Kaja drags the target toward Kadita's setup path.
failureCases: Kaja pulls the target out of Kadita's burst area.
Enemy allies body-block or counter-engage after Kaja commits.

reasons: Kaja can lock and drag one enemy into a predictable position for Pharsa's long-range burst.
Pharsa can follow Kaja's pickoff from safer distance when the target path is visible.

proof.summary: Kaja creates a controlled single-target pick, while Pharsa adds long-range damage if she has the correct angle.
worksBestWhen: Pharsa has vision of Kaja's target before casting.
Kaja drags the target into open space, not behind terrain.
failureCases: Kaja catches a target outside Pharsa's cast angle.
Pharsa needs to reposition before the damage lands.
The target escapes the repeated strike area after Kaja's suppression ends.
